notes.text:
Treatment PE17463815 seems to be the winner, quick math on impact below. It's interesting Basic is seeing the most lift, especially since we don't even assort Basic in DIME in Win32 apps. My theory there is 1) that because many of these users are in a near/critical/over-quota state and have already churned out of M365 F/P, they fall back to Basic to fulfill storage needs and 2) we are concurrently running a DIME test that personalizes for these near/critical/over quota users by showing their storage consumption, which we see driving huge lift in Basic subs. So they must be in the experiment but buying elsewhere, given Basic lift. Here's the scorecard for that looking at the top treatment and wxp win32. 
 
I think we call this a winner and follow up with a dialog customized by quota state, see if we can really lean into USQ and winback those over near/critical/quota users. (we have 21M of them in total ecosystem, then another 53M near/critical)
61k Total GAs
+40.7k F/P GAs (PSR +0.5%)
+20.3k Basic GAs (Basic purchase rate +2%)
+$11.7M LTV (+0.66%)

- Takeshi wants to do cross-promo on confirmation page on checkout
